cover_caption: Figure 2: Multiple modalities, many applications, one unified verification framework. We present LLM-as-a-Verifier, a general-purpose framework that provides fine-grained feedback for any modality without requiring additional training. By leveraging the full distribution of scoring-token logits, our method captures evaluation uncertainty and enables verification to scale along three dimensions: score granularity, repeated evaluation, and criteria decomposition. The resulting fine-grained feedback can be used for test-time scaling, progress tracking, and reinforcement learning.

figures.caption: Figure 1: Overall Performance Results. Our proposed framework, LLM-as-a-Verifier, achieves state-of-the-art performance across coding, robotics, and medical domains: Terminal-Bench V2 (86.5%), SWE-Bench Verified (78.2%), RoboRewardBench (87.4%), and MedAgentBench (73.3%).
figures.caption_ko: Figure 1: 전체 성능 결과. LLM-as-a-Verifier는 coding, robotics, medical domain 전반에서 Terminal-Bench V2 86.5%, SWE-Bench Verified 78.2%, RoboRewardBench 87.4%, MedAgentBench 73.3%로 최고 성능을 달성한다.

figures.caption: Figure 2: Multiple modalities, many applications, one unified verification framework. We present LLM-as-a-Verifier, a general-purpose framework that provides fine-grained feedback for any modality without requiring additional training. By leveraging the full distribution of scoring-token logits, our method captures evaluation uncertainty and enables verification to scale along three dimensions: score granularity, repeated evaluation, and criteria decomposition. The resulting fine-grained feedback can be used for test-time scaling, progress tracking, and reinforcement learning.
figures.caption_ko: Figure 2: 여러 modality와 application을 하나의 verification framework로 통합한다. LLM-as-a-Verifier는 별도 학습 없이 scoring-token logit 분포 전체를 이용해 불확실성을 보존하고, score granularity·반복 평가·criteria decomposition 세 축으로 검증을 확장한다. 이 세밀한 신호는 test-time scaling, progress tracking, reinforcement learning에 재사용된다.

figures.caption: Figure 3: Scaling paradigms for large language models.
figures.caption_ko: Figure 3: Large language model의 scaling paradigm. Pre-training, post-training, test-time compute에 더해 verification을 새로운 scaling 축으로 제시한다.

figures.caption: Figure 4: Verification Scaling. We find that verification accuracy consistently improves as we scale across multiple dimensions: (1) the granularity of score tokens, (2) the number of repeated evaluations, and (3) the decomposition of evaluation criteria. Verification accuracy is measured as the pairwise accuracy of the verifier in assigning a higher score to the ground-truth successful solution than to failed solutions for the same task on Terminal-Bench V2.
figures.caption_ko: Figure 4: Verification scaling. Score token의 granularity, 반복 평가 횟수, evaluation criteria 분해를 각각 늘릴수록 Terminal-Bench V2에서 성공 trajectory를 실패 trajectory보다 높게 평가하는 pairwise accuracy가 일관되게 향상된다.

figures.caption: Figure 5: Oracle Pass@K reaches 98.9% on Terminal-Bench V2.
figures.caption_ko: Figure 5: Oracle verifier가 후보를 정확히 선택할 수 있다면 Terminal-Bench V2의 Pass@K는 98.9%에 도달한다.

figures.caption: Figure 6: Probabilistic Pivot Tournament. A five-stage pipeline for selecting the best of N candidates under a constrained verification budget. (1) Candidates: the pool to be ranked. (2) Ring pass: a random Hamiltonian cycle scores the N adjacent pairs so every candidate appears once in the A slot and once in B, canceling the model's positional bias. (3) Pivot selection: candidates are ranked by their ring-pass scores, and the top-k candidates form the pivot set. (4) Pivot tournament: every non-pivot-vs-pivot and pivot-vs-pivot pair is scored, concentrating the budget on uncertain top candidates and cutting cost from O(N^2) to O(Nk). (5) Selection: comparisons are aggregated into win mass and count, and the candidate with the highest normalized score is returned.
figures.caption_ko: Figure 6: Probabilistic Pivot Tournament. 제한된 검증 예산에서 N개 후보 중 최선을 고르는 5단계 pipeline이다. 후보 구성 후 random Hamiltonian cycle의 ring pass로 각 후보를 A/B 위치에 한 번씩 배치해 positional bias를 상쇄한다. 상위 k개를 pivot으로 고르고 non-pivot–pivot 및 pivot–pivot만 비교해 비용을 O(N²)에서 O(Nk)로 줄인 뒤, 정규화된 win score가 가장 높은 후보를 반환한다.

figures.caption: Figure 7: Verifier (continuous) vs. Judge (discrete) on Terminal-Bench V2 across k in {1, 4, 16} repeated evaluations. Left: Pairwise verification accuracy. The verifier achieves 74.7% at k=1 and improves to 77.5% at k=16, consistently outperforming the judge across all evaluation budgets. Right: Tie rate. The judge produces ties in 26.7% of comparisons at k=1 due to coarse discrete scoring, decreasing to 5.5% at k=16 as averaging breaks ties. In contrast, the verifier yields zero ties.
figures.caption_ko: Figure 7: Terminal-Bench V2에서 continuous verifier와 discrete judge 비교. Verifier의 pairwise accuracy는 k=1에서 74.7%, k=16에서 77.5%로 향상되며 모든 예산에서 judge를 앞선다. Judge의 tie rate는 k=1에서 26.7%, k=16에서 5.5%지만 verifier는 모든 설정에서 tie가 0이다.

figures.caption: Figure 8: We observe a strong correlation between the chronological progression of code generation steps and the scores from LLM-as-a-Verifier. The example task above requires the agent to run MNIST inference. The successful trajectory follows a coherent sequence of events—Read model.py, Install g++ compiler, Install CPU-only torch, Update hidden_dim, DONE—and exhibits consistently increasing verifier scores. In contrast, the failed trajectory unnecessarily installs the large torchvision package, exhausts the available disk space, and hits a compilation error, resulting in significantly lower scores. Results are shown for the pytorch-model-cli task from Terminal-Bench V2, using Gemini 2.5 Pro with Terminus 2 and Gemini 2.5 Flash as the verifier.
figures.caption_ko: Figure 8: Code generation 단계의 시간적 진행과 verifier score가 강하게 상관한다. 성공 trajectory는 model.py 확인, compiler 설치, CPU-only torch 설치, hidden_dim 수정, 완료로 이어지며 점수가 상승한다. 실패 trajectory는 불필요한 torchvision 설치로 disk를 소진하고 compile error를 만나 낮은 점수를 보인다. Terminal-Bench V2의 pytorch-model-cli 결과다.

figures.caption: Figure 9: LLM-as-a-Verifier improves RL sample efficiency. Success rate versus training steps for off-policy and on-policy reinforcement learning, comparing sparse-reward baselines with dense rewards from LLM-as-a-Verifier. Left: A pi_0 policy fine-tuned on the LIBERO ketchup task with DSRL-SAC. The verifier progress reward achieves the same success rate using approximately 1.8x fewer environment steps and reaches a higher final success rate (0.76 vs. 0.69). Right: Qwen3-8B fine-tuned on MATH with GRPO. The verifier reasoning reward improves sample efficiency by approximately 1.1x. Results are averaged over multiple seeds (LIBERO n=5, MATH n=3).
figures.caption_ko: Figure 9: LLM-as-a-Verifier가 RL sample efficiency를 높인다. LIBERO ketchup task에서 pi_0를 DSRL-SAC로 fine-tuning할 때 verifier progress reward는 약 1.8배 적은 environment step으로 같은 성공률에 도달하고 최종 성공률도 0.69에서 0.76으로 높인다. MATH의 Qwen3-8B GRPO에서는 reasoning reward가 sample efficiency를 약 1.1배 개선한다.

figures.caption: Figure 10: Trajectory-preference accuracy improves consistently as the number of repeated evaluations k increases, rising from 81.5% at k=1 to 87.4% at k=8. LLM-as-a-Verifier outperforms LLM-as-a-Judge and prior reward models (TOPReward, RoboReward-8B, Robometer-4B) across all budgets, with gains saturating at larger k.
figures.caption_ko: Figure 10: 반복 평가 횟수 k가 늘수록 RoboRewardBench trajectory-preference accuracy가 k=1의 81.5%에서 k=8의 87.4%로 일관되게 향상된다. 모든 예산에서 LLM-as-a-Judge, TOPReward, RoboReward-8B, Robometer-4B를 앞서며 큰 k에서는 이득이 포화한다.

tables.caption: Table 1: Signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). The SNR measures how reliably the verifier separates correct from incorrect trajectories. As the number of scoring tokens G increases, the SNR grows, indicating better-calibrated score separation.
tables.caption_ko: Table 1: Signal-to-noise ratio. SNR은 verifier가 correct trajectory와 incorrect trajectory를 얼마나 안정적으로 분리하는지 측정한다. Scoring token 수 G가 늘수록 SNR이 증가해 score separation이 더 잘 calibration됨을 보여준다.

tables.caption: Table 2: Judges vs. Verifiers on query-optimize. Over 100 repeated evaluations, the discrete 1–5 judge produces ties in 88/100 evaluations. Taking the expectation over the same 1–5 scale eliminates ties and correctly ranks the trajectory in 69/100 evaluations. Increasing score granularity to G=20 further improves discrimination, correctly ranking the trajectory in 77/100 evaluations.
tables.caption_ko: Table 2: query-optimize에서 judge와 verifier 비교. 100회 평가에서 discrete 1–5 judge는 88회 tie를 만든다. 같은 1–5 분포의 기대값을 사용하면 tie가 사라지고 69회 정답 trajectory를 높게 평가한다. Granularity를 G=20으로 늘리면 77회로 향상된다.

tables.caption: Table 3: Per-benchmark performance and gains from verification. Baseline accuracies are obtained under a fixed agent harness. On the same candidate pools, the table reports Pass@1, the oracle Pass@N upper bound, and the accuracy achieved by LLM-as-a-Verifier. The method consistently improves over Pass@1 and recovers a large portion of the oracle headroom, achieving state-of-the-art performance on each benchmark.
tables.caption_ko: Table 3: Benchmark별 성능과 verification 이득. 같은 candidate pool에서 Pass@1, oracle Pass@N 상한, LLM-as-a-Verifier 정확도를 비교한다. 모든 benchmark에서 Pass@1을 높이고 oracle headroom의 상당 부분을 회수해 최고 성능을 달성한다.

tables.caption: Table 4: Preference accuracy on RoboRewardBench. LLM-as-a-Verifier outperforms trained robotics reward models.
tables.caption_ko: Table 4: RoboRewardBench preference accuracy. LLM-as-a-Verifier가 학습된 robotics reward model들을 능가한다.

tables.caption: Table 5: Evaluation on RoboRewardBench against human annotations. The table reports Mean Absolute Error (MAE; lower is better) between human labels and predicted rewards. LLM-as-a-Verifier uses continuous rewards with K=8, whereas the baseline extracts discrete scores from the model.
tables.caption_ko: Table 5: RoboRewardBench human annotation 대비 평가. Human label과 predicted reward 사이의 MAE를 비교하며 낮을수록 좋다. LLM-as-a-Verifier는 K=8의 continuous reward를 사용하고 baseline은 discrete score를 추출한다.

tables.caption: Table 6: Value-Order Correlation by trajectory outcome on Terminal-Bench V2. Mean Spearman rank correlation between step index and verifier progress score is computed over 500 randomly sampled trajectories. The verifier exhibits near-monotonic progress for successful trajectories, while failed rollouts show weaker correlation, producing a 0.08 Spearman gap.
tables.caption_ko: Table 6: Terminal-Bench V2 trajectory outcome별 Value-Order Correlation. 500개 trajectory에서 step index와 verifier progress score의 평균 Spearman correlation을 측정한다. 성공 rollout은 거의 단조롭게 진행하지만 실패 rollout의 상관은 더 약해 0.08의 gap이 생긴다.

tables.caption: Table 7: Value-Order Correlation on 500 trajectories from RoboRewardBench. LLM-as-a-Verifier with K=5 repeated evaluations and G=20 scoring granularity attains the highest rank correlation between chronological step index and predicted progress score.
tables.caption_ko: Table 7: RoboRewardBench 500개 trajectory의 Value-Order Correlation. K=5 반복 평가와 G=20 granularity를 사용한 LLM-as-a-Verifier가 시간 순서와 예측 progress score 사이에서 가장 높은 rank correlation을 얻는다.

tables.caption: Table 8: Harness generalization on Terminal-Bench V2. LLM-as-a-Verifier significantly boosts accuracy on two additional harnesses, reaching 71.2% on Terminus-2 and 79.4% on Terminus-Kira.
tables.caption_ko: Table 8: Terminal-Bench V2 harness generalization. LLM-as-a-Verifier는 추가 harness에서도 Terminus-2 71.2%, Terminus-Kira 79.4%로 정확도를 크게 높인다.

tables.caption: Table 9: Probabilistic Pivot Tournament scales with the number of pivots, achieving higher accuracy as k increases while maintaining a low verification budget on Terminal-Bench V2.
tables.caption_ko: Table 9: Probabilistic Pivot Tournament은 pivot 수 k를 늘릴수록 정확도가 높아지면서도 Terminal-Bench V2의 verification budget을 낮게 유지한다.

tables.caption: Table 10: LLM-as-a-Verifier as a process reward model. Per-step verification increases pass@1 monotonically as the number of sampled actions per step grows.
tables.caption_ko: Table 10: Process reward model로서의 LLM-as-a-Verifier. Step마다 sampling하는 action 수가 늘어날수록 per-step verification의 pass@1이 단조롭게 증가한다.

tables.caption: Table 11: LLM-as-a-Verifier as an outcome reward model improves pass@1 accuracy across coding and mathematics benchmarks. Under Best-of-N sampling, it improves over the base model by 9.5% on SWE-Bench Lite, 18.5% on AIME, and 21.3% on HMMT while using a smaller verification budget than V1.
tables.caption_ko: Table 11: Outcome reward model로서 coding과 mathematics benchmark의 pass@1을 개선한다. Best-of-N에서 base model 대비 SWE-Bench Lite 9.5%p, AIME 18.5%p, HMMT 21.3%p 향상하면서 V1보다 적은 verification budget을 사용한다.

tables.caption: Table 12: Accuracy and tie rate on Terminal-Bench V2 as repeated evaluations K scale from 1 to 16. GPT-5.5 Discrete averages integer 1–10 scores, while GPT-5.5 to Gemini 2.5 Flash Continuous forwards the closed-model reasoning to an open verifier and reads continuous rewards from scoring-token logits.
tables.caption_ko: Table 12: Terminal-Bench V2에서 반복 평가 K를 1에서 16까지 늘릴 때 accuracy와 tie rate. GPT-5.5 Discrete는 정수 1–10 score를 평균하고, continuous 방식은 GPT-5.5 reasoning을 Gemini 2.5 Flash로 전달해 scoring-token logit에서 연속 reward를 읽는다.

relations.note: Both address scalable oversight of increasingly capable agent systems; this paper supplies a general-purpose fine-grained verifier rather than an automated research organization.
Both treat test-time computation over multiple candidate agent behaviors as a scaling axis, with verification and orchestration as complementary selection mechanisms.
ENPIRE depends on automatic verification in physical policy-improvement loops, while LLM-as-a-Verifier provides a transferable continuous progress and reward signal.
Both rank candidate agent outputs through tournaments; the AI co-scientist uses Elo debate while this work uses probabilistic pairwise scores and pivots.

ai_summary.one_liner: LLM-as-a-Verifier turns scoring-token logit distributions into continuous trajectory scores and scales verification through granularity, repetition, and criteria decomposition, reaching state of the art across coding, robotics, and medical agent benchmarks without verifier training.
ai_summary.problem: Discrete LLM judges collapse uncertain scoring distributions into coarse tokens, causing ties and weak discrimination, while trained reward models often fail to generalize across domains and exhaustive candidate comparison is costly.
ai_summary.solution: Compute the expected value over score-token probabilities, average across repeated evaluations and decomposed criteria, convert continuous rewards into Bradley-Terry preferences, and rank candidates with an O(Nk) Probabilistic Pivot Tournament.
ai_summary.key_result: The framework reaches 86.5% on Terminal-Bench V2, 78.2% on SWE-Bench Verified, 87.4% on RoboRewardBench, and 73.3% on MedAgentBench; it also yields VOC 0.966 on robot trajectories and improves LIBERO RL sample efficiency by about 1.8x.
limitations: Direct continuous scoring requires access to scoring-token log probabilities, which several closed frontier APIs do not expose.
Criteria are manually designed and repeated evaluations allocate compute uniformly rather than according to uncertainty.
Dense-reward experiments are limited to single-turn settings rather than long-horizon multi-turn reinforcement learning.
Repeated sampling reduces variance but cannot remove correlated verifier bias or systematic model-family blind spots.

research_gaps.question: How can continuous verification retain its calibration when frontier model APIs expose reasoning and sampled tokens but not scoring-token log probabilities?
Can evaluation criteria and repeated-compute budgets be generated and allocated adaptively from the verifier's own uncertainty instead of being hand-designed and uniform?
Does dense verifier feedback remain stable for multi-turn RL and long-horizon agent trajectories where early scoring errors alter all later states?
How robust is probabilistic verification to correlated bias, adversarial trajectories, and reward hacking when generation and verification models share training data or model families?

future_directions: Learn or dynamically generate domain-specific criteria rather than relying on manually authored rubrics.
Allocate repeated evaluations and pivots adaptively using estimated verifier uncertainty.
Develop calibrated continuous-scoring interfaces for closed models without requiring a second open verifier.
Extend dense verification rewards from single-turn experiments to multi-turn, long-horizon reinforcement learning.
Measure and mitigate correlated verifier bias, adversarial gaming, and cross-model-family calibration drift.
ai_research_question: Can verification become an independent scaling axis that selects, monitors, and trains agent trajectories more efficiently than simply scaling the generator?
